definition: Continue in a course of action even in the face of difficulty
example: If you're going through hell, keep going and persevere.

definition: A great victory or achievement
example: We shall fight on the beaches, we shall fight on the landing grounds, we shall never surrender in triumph.

definition: Firm determination to do something
example: Never give in, never, never, never, in nothing great or small, large or petty, never give in except to convictions of honor and good sense.

definition: Difficulties; misfortune
example: If you're going through hell, keep going through adversity.

definition: The ability to do something that frightens one
example: Courage is what it takes to stand up and speak; courage is also what it takes to sit down and listen.

definition: The events that will necessarily happen to a particular person or thing
example: It is not the end. It is not even the beginning of the end. But it is, perhaps, the end of the beginning of our destiny.

definition: The quality of being determined; firmness of purpose
example: We shall fight on the beaches, we shall fight on the landing grounds, we shall never surrender with determination.

definition: The capacity to recover quickly from difficulties
example: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts as resilience.

definition: The quality or fact of being very determined; determination
example: Continuous effort - not strength or intelligence - is the key to unlocking your potential through tenacity.

definition: An act of defeating an enemy or opponent in a battle, game, or other competition
example: Victory at all costs, victory in spite of all terror, victory however long and hard the road may be.

definition: The armistice agreement signed between the Allies and Germany on 11 November 1918 to end World War I; (by extension) the end of World War I.
example: The speech returned to the idea of armistice as a guiding principle.

definition: An extremely cruel act; a horrid act of injustice.
example: The regime is guilty of mass atrocities including forced displacement and the use of chemical weapons.

definition: In warfare, an agreed end to hostilities for a specific purpose. (Typically only temporary).
example: The rebels agreed to a ceasefire while the peace talks were underway.

definition: The easing of hostility or tension between rivals
example: They treated detente as a fragile opening that still required vigilance.

definition: A person, such as an ambassador, who is accredited to represent a government officially in its relations with other governments or international organisations
example: The speech returned to the idea of diplomat as a guiding principle.

definition: An authoritarian political system marked by dictatorial power and suppression of opposition
example: He warned that fascism grows when fear silences public courage.

definition: To increase the defenses of; to strengthen and secure by military works; to render defensible against an attack by hostile forces.
example: The speaker tried to fortify the audience with a calm but forceful appeal.

definition: The systematic killing of substantial numbers of people on the basis of their ethnicity, religion, political beliefs, social status, or other particularities.
example: A genocide will always be followed by the denial that it ever happened.

definition: Concerned with improving people's lives and reducing suffering
example: The address made a humanitarian case for protecting civilians.

definition: Doctrine, philosophy, body of beliefs or principles belonging to an individual or group.
example: A dictatorship, in order to secure its reign, bans things that do not conform to its ideology.

definition: The state of being infamous.
example: The speech returned to the idea of infamy as a guiding principle.

definition: Rebellion; revolt; the state of being insurgent
example: suppress the insurgency by isolating the rebels from the rest of the population

definition: The act of liberating or the state of being liberated.
example: The speech returned to the idea of liberation as a guiding principle.

definition: To make something mobile.
example: The speaker tried to mobilize the audience with a calm but forceful appeal.

definition: The capacity of people to maintain belief in an institution or a goal, or even in oneself and others.
example: Morale is an important quality in soldiers. With good morale they'll charge into a hail of bullets; without it they won't even cross a street.

definition: An activity or task with which one occupies oneself; usually specifically the productive activity, service, trade, or craft for which one is regularly paid; a job.
example: The speech returned to the idea of occupation as a guiding principle.

definition: The exercise of authority or power in a burdensome, cruel, or unjust manner.
example: The speech returned to the idea of oppression as a guiding principle.

definition: Love of one's country; devotion to the welfare of one's compatriots; passion which inspires one to serve one's country.
example: The speech returned to the idea of patriotism as a guiding principle.

definition: A principle of behaviour, conduct etc. thought to be desirable or necessary, especially as formally expressed by a government or other authoritative body.
example: The Communist Party has a policy of returning power to the workers.

definition: An act in the past which may be used as an example to help decide the outcome of similar instances in the future.
example: The speech returned to the idea of precedent as a guiding principle.

definition: A concerted set of messages aimed at influencing the opinions or behavior of large numbers of people.
example: The speech returned to the idea of propaganda as a guiding principle.

definition: The reestablishment of friendly relations; conciliation or rapprochement.
example: He longed for reconciliation with his estranged father, but too many painful memories kept him from making contact again.

definition: The act or process of regaining or repossession of something lost.
example: The speech returned to the idea of recovery as a guiding principle.

definition: A state of safety, protection or shelter.
example: The speech returned to the idea of refuge as a guiding principle.

definition: An approval, by an authority, generally one that makes something valid.
example: The whalers had been operating in the contested waters off the island with sanction from the Japanese government.

definition: The condition of not being threatened, especially physically, psychologically, emotionally, or financially.
example: The speech returned to the idea of security as a guiding principle.

definition: A man who is a leader in national or international affairs.
example: The speech returned to the idea of statesman as a guiding principle.

definition: A peak; the topmost point or surface, as of a mountain.
example: In summer, it is possible to hike to the summit of Mount Shasta.

definition: An act of surrendering, submission into the possession of another; abandonment, resignation.
example: The speech returned to the idea of surrender as a guiding principle.

definition: An advocate of totalitarianism.
example: The speech returned to the idea of totalitarian as a guiding principle.

definition: A binding agreement concluded by subjects of international law, namely states and international organizations.
example: The speech returned to the idea of treaty as a guiding principle.

definition: A state of great disorder or uncertainty.
example: The speech returned to the idea of turmoil as a guiding principle.

definition: Change, from one state to another
example: The speech returned to the idea of upheaval as a guiding principle.

definition: Alert watchfulness.
example: The speech returned to the idea of vigilance as a guiding principle.

definition: Connected with a period during which a war is being fought
example: The wartime broadcast steadied a frightened nation.

definition: The act of unifying.
example: The speech returned to the idea of unification as a guiding principle.

definition: An act of retaliation.
example: The speech returned to the idea of reprisal as a guiding principle.

definition: The condition of being stable or in equilibrium, and thus resistant to change.
example: The speech returned to the idea of stability as a guiding principle.

definition: The act of resisting, or the capacity to resist.
example: the resistance of bacteria to certain antibiotics

definition: The act of deterring, or the state of being deterred.
example: The speech returned to the idea of deterrence as a guiding principle.

definition: Someone whose job is to speak for someone's case in a court of law; a counsel.
example: The speech returned to the idea of advocate as a guiding principle.

definition: The state of being allied.
example: matrimonial alliances; an alliance between church and state, or between two countries

definition: To render larger, more extended, or more intense.
example: amplify a microscopes

definition: To make suitable; to make to correspond; to fit or suit
example: The speaker tried to adapt the audience with a calm but forceful appeal.

definition: A state in which opposing forces harmonise; equilibrium.
example: The speech returned to the idea of balance as a guiding principle.

definition: A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed in the process.
example: The speech returned to the idea of catalyst as a guiding principle.

definition: A confrontation; a dare.
example: The speech returned to the idea of challenge as a guiding principle.

definition: A being involved in the action of a story.
example: The speech returned to the idea of character as a guiding principle.

definition: An option; a decision; an opportunity to choose or select something.
example: Do I have a choice of what color to paint it?

definition: The state or measure of being clear, either in appearance, thought or style; lucidity.
example: Lack of clarity on the part of the teacher will cause confusion among the students.

definition: A temporary group or union of organizations, usually formed for a particular advantage.
example: The Liberal Democrats and Conservative parties formed a coalition government in 2010.

definition: A group sharing a common understanding, and often the same language, law, manners, and/or tradition.
example: The speech returned to the idea of community as a guiding principle.

definition: A process of decision-making that seeks widespread agreement among group members.
example: The speech returned to the idea of consensus as a guiding principle.

definition: A firmly held belief.
example: The speech returned to the idea of conviction as a guiding principle.

definition: Believable or plausible.
example: Her credible tone gave the address an unmistakable sense of purpose.

definition: An argument, or discussion, usually in an ordered or formal setting, often with more than two people, generally ending with a vote or other decision.
example: After a four-hour debate, the committee voted to table the motion.

definition: A conversation or other form of discourse between two or more individuals.
example: Bill and Melinda maintained a dialogue via email over the course of their long-distance relationship.

definition: The state of being dignified or worthy of esteem: elevation of mind or character.
example: The speech returned to the idea of dignity as a guiding principle.

definition: Verbal exchange, conversation.
example: The speech returned to the idea of discourse as a guiding principle.

definition: Identification with or understanding of the thoughts, feelings, or emotional state of another person.
example: She had a lot of empathy for her neighbor; she knew what it was like to lose a parent too.

definition: A sincere attempt; a determined or assiduous effort towards a specific goal; assiduous or persistent activity.
example: The speech returned to the idea of endeavor as a guiding principle.

definition: To continue or carry on, despite obstacles or hardships; to persist.
example: The singer's popularity endured for decades.

definition: Fairness, impartiality, or justice as determined in light of 'natural law' or 'natural right'.
example: The speech returned to the idea of equity as a guiding principle.

definition: The study of principles relating to right and wrong conduct.
example: The speech returned to the idea of ethics as a guiding principle.

definition: Facts or observations presented in support of an assertion.
example: There is no evidence that anyone was here earlier.

definition: The property of being fair or equitable.
example: Some questioned the fairness of the new laws.

definition: Mental or emotional strength that enables courage in the face of adversity.
example: The speech returned to the idea of fortitude as a guiding principle.

definition: The power or right to act, speak, or think without unjust restraint
example: The speech framed freedom as the birthright of every citizen.

definition: The time ahead; those moments yet to be experienced.
example: The speech returned to the idea of future as a guiding principle.

definition: The act of governing
example: The speech returned to the idea of govern as a guiding principle.

definition: Someone who guides, especially someone hired to show people around a place or an institution and offer information and explanation.
example: The guide led us around the museum and explained the exhibits.

definition: Agreement or accord.
example: The speech returned to the idea of harmony as a guiding principle.

definition: A spell or ability that restores hit points or removes a status ailment.
example: The speech returned to the idea of heal as a guiding principle.

definition: The characteristic of being humble; humbleness in character and behavior.
example: The speech returned to the idea of humility as a guiding principle.

definition: The striking of one body against another; collision.
example: The speech returned to the idea of impact as a guiding principle.

definition: An addition or annex to a group, set, or total.
example: The poem was a new inclusion in the textbook.

definition: The power to affect, control or manipulate something or someone; the ability to change the development of fluctuating things such as conduct, thoughts or decisions.
example: I have absolutely no influence over him.

definition: To infuse into the mind; to communicate to the spirit; to convey, as by a divine or supernatural influence; to disclose preternaturally; to produce in, as by inspiration.
example: The speaker tried to inspire the audience with a calm but forceful appeal.

definition: Steadfast adherence to a strict moral or ethical code.
example: The speech returned to the idea of integrity as a guiding principle.

definition: The state or characteristic of being just or fair.
example: the justice of a description

definition: Money or property bequeathed to someone in a will.
example: The speech returned to the idea of legacy as a guiding principle.

definition: The condition of being free from control or restrictions.
example: The army is here, your liberty is assured.

definition: An instance of listening.
example: Give the motor a listen and tell me if it sounds off.

definition: An official or authoritative command; an order or injunction; a commission; a judicial precept.
example: The speech returned to the idea of mandate as a guiding principle.

definition: Of a body in motion: the tendency of a body to maintain its inertial motion; the product of its mass and velocity.
example: The speech returned to the idea of momentum as a guiding principle.

definition: (of a narrative) The ethical significance or practical lesson.
example: The speech returned to the idea of moral as a guiding principle.

definition: A minor distinction.
example: The speech returned to the idea of nuance as a guiding principle.

definition: A chance for advancement, progress or profit.
example: The world is full of opportunities and it's up to me to see them and pursue them.

definition: A tendency to expect the best, or at least, a favourable outcome
example: I love her youth, her beauty and above all her optimism that everything will turn out fine.

definition: A state of tranquility, quiet, and harmony; absence of violence. For instance, a state free from civil disturbance.
example: Our lounge strives to maintain an environment of peace for the comfort of our customers.
